functions.name: initialize_array_with_constructor
functions.description: Walks `count` entries from `base` using the supplied byte stride and calls the provided constructor/helper on each slot; shared by game-runtime, enemy-manager, and parcel-bucket pool setup.

functions.name: draw_frontend_widget
functions.description: Renders one shell-font front-end widget, including nine-slice border styles, slider-track strips, optional glow overlay, icon sprites, and the final text draw.

functions.name: initialize_frontend_sprite_button
functions.description: Constructs one sprite-backed front-end child control, including arrow-button animation ids, authored anchor, and immediate-vs-delayed click behavior.

functions.name: initialize_frontend_widget
functions.description: Constructs one shell-font front-end widget, seeds its border style, text scale, alignment, colors, and optional slider-child controls, then links it into the active widget list.

functions.name: layout_frontend_widget
functions.description: Recomputes one front-end widget's authored-space position, measured text bounds, and any attached slider-child offsets before the current frame draw.

functions.name: set_frontend_widget_shortcut_key
functions.description: Stores one keyboard shortcut code on a front-end widget and enables the matching flag so `update_frontend_widget_interaction` dispatches the widget when `read_pressed_text_input_key_code()` returns that code.

functions.name: stack_widget_below
functions.description: Chains one shell-font front-end widget below another by copying the previous widget x anchor and adding the current gap plus the previous measured height.

functions.name: unhighlight_border
functions.description: Clears the active highlight state on one frontend border/widget and restores its non-highlighted visual bits.

functions.name: highlight_border
functions.description: Sets the active highlight state on one frontend border/widget so hover/selection visuals render in the highlighted style.

functions.name: update_frontend_widget_interaction
functions.description: Runs one front-end widget's per-frame interaction state, including hover easing, delayed click-bit dispatch, slider-arrow adjustments, bounds refresh, and draw.

functions.name: queue_frontend_widget_flag_after_delay
functions.description: Arms the shared border-manager delay lane for one front-end widget: stores the destination widget pointer and flag mask, starts the fixed `0.1` step timer, and optionally begins the global front-end fade when the widget requests it.

functions.name: flush_row_event_display
functions.description: Fast-forwards the active gameplay row-event display by paying out any remaining row score awards, destroying its widget actors, and clearing the owning controller state.

functions.name: initialize_completion_screen
functions.description: Builds the Delivery Complete screen, seeds its package-count and bonus summary widgets, and lays out the continue prompt. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRCompletion::Init(int, bool)`.

functions.name: update_row_event_display
functions.description: Runs the gameplay row-event display state machine, including staged score payout, widget show or hide transitions, and the final handoff into `flush_row_event_display`.

functions.name: register_parcel_delivery
functions.description: Records one delivered parcel on the gameplay row-event display controller, awards the per-parcel score event, and dispatches the all-parcels-delivered bonus when the final parcel arrives.

functions.name: classify_archive_entry_extension
functions.description: Copies one archive entry's filename stem before the first dot into the caller buffer and returns the rebuild classifier id: `1` for `TGA`, `2` for `WAV`, `3` for `MP3`, otherwise `0`.

functions.name: get_stream_length_preserve_position
functions.description: Seeks one open file stream to the end, reads its byte length, and restores the original stream position before returning.

functions.name: load_file_bytes_allocating
functions.description: Loads one filesystem file into a freshly allocated byte buffer and optionally returns the byte length through the out parameter.

functions.name: load_file_bytes_into_optional_buffer
functions.description: Loads one filesystem file into the caller-supplied buffer when present, otherwise allocates a new buffer, and optionally returns the byte length through the out parameter.

functions.name: scramble_archive_bytes_in_place
functions.description: Applies the archive rebuild byte-scramble in place by XORing each byte with its index-derived polynomial mask.

functions.name: save_file_bytes_with_optional_archive_scramble
functions.description: Writes one byte buffer to the filesystem and optionally applies the archive rebuild scramble before the write.

functions.name: delete_file_path
functions.description: Deletes one filesystem path; used by the archive rebuild pass to remove the old archive and temporary extracted PNG.

functions.name: toggle_archive_high_bit_in_place
functions.description: Toggles bit 7 on each byte in place; used to decode the legacy `.dam` archive payload before rebuild.

functions.name: rebuild_game_archive_if_needed
functions.description: When `SnailMail.dat` is missing but `SnailMail.dam` exists, decodes the legacy archive, repacks TGA entries through the image loader, copies other entries unchanged, writes the rebuilt `SnailMail.dat`, and deletes the old `.dam` plus temporary `0.png`.

functions.name: load_x_mesh
functions.description: Loads one DirectX X mesh into an owned Object: parses authored 16-bit vertex/face counts, allocates vertices and facequads, writes scalar UV and index lanes through the Object-owned face array, and resolves per-face TextureRef material pointers.

functions.name: load_or_reuse_cached_x_mesh
functions.description: Searches the shared runtime X-mesh bank by name and returns the existing index on a hit; on a miss it appends a new entry, allocates its backing object from the global object list, loads the mesh, and returns the new index.

functions.name: load_x_animation_clip
functions.description: Enumerates one mesh's X-animation keyframes into shared 0x80-byte BodBase-derived records, loads their borrowed ObjectList slots, parses animation duration/mode metadata, and asks the destination Object to retain the generated animation graph.

functions.name: destroy_completion_screen
functions.description: Tears down the active completion-screen widgets and restores the owning front-end state once the current exit choice resolves. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRCompletion::UnInit()`.

functions.name: initialize_exit_prompt
functions.description: Builds the shared Yes/No exit prompt used by the front-end completion return flow, including the `Do you really want to quit?` title and the per-owner button layout variants. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRExit::Init()`.

functions.name: update_completion_screen
functions.description: Runs the completion-screen state machine, including continue, replay, route-map return, and outbound-link actions. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRCompletion::AI()`.

functions.name: initialize_default_runtime_config
functions.description: Seeds the 0xc4-byte SnailMail.cfg blob with default sound volume, music volume, fullscreen, progression, and tuning values before any file overlay is loaded.

functions.name: initialize_main_loop_display_state
functions.description: Resets the display-mode/view-sample state, window deactivation latches, object texture transform matrix, and main-loop timing counters before startup enters the active frame loop.

functions.name: handle_game_window_activate
functions.description: Runtime reactivation helper for the main window. It clears the inactive flag, reapplies fullscreen mode when configured, refreshes the frame timestamp, shows and focuses the game window, and clears the mouse button latch bytes after WM_ACTIVATE enters the active state.

functions.name: show_and_focus_game_window
functions.description: Startup-only helper that shows the game window, brings it to the foreground, sets focus and active window state, refreshes the frame timestamp, and clears the mouse button latch bytes before the main loop begins.

functions.name: handle_game_window_deactivate
functions.description: Runtime deactivation helper for the main window. When the game is active in fullscreen mode, it marks the window inactive, minimizes it, and clears the pending quit flag before returning.

functions.name: game_window_proc
functions.description: Primary Win32 window procedure for the game's main window. It handles activation and minimize transitions, ESC-to-quit, left and right mouse button latches, and mouse-wheel delta capture before delegating the rest to DefWindowProcA.

functions.name: consume_mouse_wheel_delta
functions.description: Returns the latched mouse-wheel delta for the requested slot and clears it back to zero.

functions.name: read_left_mouse_button_state
functions.description: Clears the companion left-button latch byte for the requested slot and returns the current left mouse button state byte.

functions.name: read_right_mouse_button_state
functions.description: Clears the companion right-button latch byte for the requested slot and returns the current right mouse button state byte.

functions.name: snapshot_current_display_mode
functions.description: Reads the current desktop display mode into the shared `DEVMODEA` buffer at `data_4df868` before fullscreen or pointer-scaling setup.

functions.name: restore_desktop_display_mode
functions.description: Restores the desktop display mode with ChangeDisplaySettingsA(nullptr, 0) and makes the OS cursor visible again. The startup loop and deactivate path use it when leaving fullscreen presentation.

functions.name: probe_display_mode_count
functions.description: Walks `EnumDisplaySettingsA` until it fails and returns the terminal result, which the startup path uses while gathering desktop display capabilities.

functions.name: read_current_display_resolution
functions.description: Reads the current desktop width and height into the supplied outputs, falling back to `640x480` if `EnumDisplaySettingsA` fails.

functions.name: clear_display_mode_state
functions.description: Clears the queued display-mode view-sample count and current display-mode pointer.

functions.name: reset_display_mode_probe_count
functions.description: Clears the display-mode probe count after the main window restores from minimization.

functions.name: read_next_display_mode_view_sample
functions.description: Returns the next display-mode view sample or the current mode dimensions during the startup probe warm-up.

functions.name: noop_runtime_ai
functions.description: Shared empty per-frame update stub reused by runtime object types that do not need standalone AI work. Cross-port Android symbol matches tie this address to multiple `::AI()` slots including camera, weapon, and ball-style objects.

functions.name: construct_game_runtime
functions.description: Allocates the `cRGame` root object, seeds its embedded managers and callback tables, initializes the runtime pools, and publishes the live global game pointer at `data_4df904`.

functions.name: initialize_game_player
functions.description: Constructs one 0x1f8-byte Windows cRPlayer record: initializes its inherited renderable body, constructs the owned cRCamera subobject at +0xa0, constructs two owned colors at +0x188/+0x198, and installs the cRPlayer::AI callback.

functions.name: initialize_noop_renderable_bod
functions.description: Constructs one RenderableBod and replaces its callback with the shared noop_runtime_ai slot; construct_game_runtime uses it for the three passive overlay child bodies.

functions.name: initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank
functions.description: Initializes the embedded subgame pools, including exactly 126 0xa8-byte path-template records at SubgameRuntime+0xff2914. Those records form the same 63 primary/secondary pairs later constructed through the GameRoot+0x1066f2c alias; their 0x52b0 extent ends at the barrier actor at +0xff7bc4.

functions.name: initialize_border_record
functions.description: Constructs one 0x724-byte front-end border/widget pool record by seeding the shared BOD header, no-op-constructing the constructor-proven color/style blocks, and installing the front-end widget callback table. `construct_game_runtime` uses it for the 150-entry `BorderManager::borders` pool at `game + 0x11d0`.

functions.name: initialize_cached_x_mesh_slot
functions.description: Constructs one 0xbc-byte cached X-mesh object slot by seeding the shared BOD header and installing the cache-slot callback table. The slot bank starts at `game + 0x48e08`; `load_or_reuse_cached_x_mesh(game + 0x48e00, ...)` stores names at slot `+0x3c` and object BOD refs at slot `+0x24`.

functions.name: initialize_intro_logo_renderable
functions.description: Constructs one 0x90-byte intro/logo renderable by running the shared renderable-BOD constructor and installing the callback table whose first entry is `update_intro_logo_renderable`. `construct_game_runtime` seeds both the 1024-entry intro/credits text bank and the 32-entry logo row bank with this constructor.

functions.name: initialize_track_speedup_runtime
functions.description: Constructs the shared speed-up runtime record by running the shared renderable-body constructor and installing the speed-up vtable. Even though `spawn_track_speedup` is an empty stub in this build, the same `0x355db0` runtime lane is still consumed by `update_jetpack_gauge`, `handle_subgoldy_collisions`, and `remove_subgame_bods`, and its `0xb4` footprint matches the native `cRSubSpeedUp` size ledger.

functions.name: initialize_track_jetpack_pickup_runtime
functions.description: Constructs the single jetpack-pickup runtime record by seeding its shared bod header plus two embedded renderable bodies. `spawn_track_jetpack_pickup` uses the resulting `game->jetpack_pickup` lane at `0x355e64`, whose `0x19c` footprint matches the native `cRJetPack` size ledger.

functions.name: initialize_track_health_pickup_runtime
functions.description: Constructs one health-pickup runtime slot by seeding the shared bod header and installing the pickup vtable. `spawn_track_health_pickup` allocates from the 8-slot pool at `0x356000`, whose total `0x3a0` footprint matches the native `cRSubHealth` size ledger.

functions.name: initialize_slug_hazard_runtime
functions.description: Constructs one slug-hazard runtime slot by running the shared renderable-body constructor and installing the slug vtable. `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` uses it to seed the 8-slot slug bank whose total footprint matches the native `cRSlug` size ledger.

functions.name: initialize_garbage_hazard
functions.description: Constructs one garbage-hazard runtime object by running the shared body constructor and installing the cRSubGarbage vtable. `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` uses it to seed the 50-slot garbage pool that later feeds `spawn_track_garbage_hazard`.

functions.name: initialize_track_ring_or_special_effect_runtime
functions.description: Constructs one authored-ring runtime slot by seeding the shared bod header and installing the ring-or-special-effect vtable. `spawn_track_ring_or_special_effect` allocates from the 2-slot pool at `0x35b78c` before initializing the child particle sprites.

functions.name: initialize_track_row_runtime
functions.description: Constructs one runtime track-row record by seeding its shared bod header, installing the row vtable on the embedded renderable body, and clearing the per-row auxiliary bod. `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` uses it for the `0x5ccac8 + row * 0xf4` bank documented as the native runtime row grid.

functions.name: initialize_path_template_record_pair
functions.description: Constructs one 0xa8-byte path-template record by initializing its leading BodBase and embedded fringe BodBase at +0x60, then installs the shared callback table. The retained historical name says pair, but the primary/secondary path pair consists of two adjacent calls/records.

functions.name: initialize_active_bod
functions.description: Initializes one shared active-bod runtime header by clearing its list links and installing the common subtype callback table at `data_497338`.

functions.name: noop_runtime_slot_constructor
functions.description: No-op thiscall constructor callback (`mov eax, ecx; ret`) used by `initialize_array_with_constructor` for POD-like runtime slot arrays and enemy-manager I/O stream members.

functions.name: initialize_sub_lazer_runtime
functions.description: Constructs one laser-projectile runtime slot by running the shared renderable-body constructor and installing the laser vtable. The 20-slot pool built by `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` totals `0xdc0`, matching the native `cRSubLazerManager` size ledger.

functions.name: initialize_salt_hazard_runtime
functions.description: Constructs one salt-hazard runtime slot by running the shared body constructor and installing the salt-hazard vtable. `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` uses it to seed the 40-slot array later reset by `initialize_salt_hazard_pool`.

functions.name: initialize_fringe_object
functions.description: Constructs one 0x38-byte fringe render object by clearing the shared object base and installing the fringe-object vtable. `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` uses it to seed the 7000-entry fringe-manager pool later consumed by `allocate_fringe_object`.

functions.name: initialize_click_start_controller_runtime
functions.description: Constructs the renderable click-start controller and installs the callback table whose first entry is `update_click_start`. This is the runtime slot constructor; `initialize_click_start` later seeds the gameplay prompt state.

functions.name: initialize_golb_shot
functions.description: Constructs one 0x2e8-byte Golb shot slot by seeding its three renderable-body prefixes and installing the `update_golb_ai`, vapour, and no-op child callback tables. `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` uses it for the 12-shot bank later driven by `create_golb`.

functions.name: initialize_enemy_manager_runtime
functions.description: Constructs the embedded enemy-manager runtime object by seeding its visible bods and child renderable entries before the later gameplay reset in `initialize_enemy_manager`. Its `0x1804` footprint matches the native `cREnemyManager` size ledger from `construct_game_runtime`.

functions.name: initialize_active_landscape_entry
functions.description: Constructs one active landscape-entry runtime record by clearing its shared body-list links and installing the landscape-entry update selector. `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` uses it to seed the 10-slot pool later driven by `activate_landscape_entry`.

functions.name: initialize_landscape_script_record
functions.description: Constructs one 0x124-byte parsed landscape-script record by running the no-op `Color4f` constructor for its `fog_color` field at `+0x110`; used for the 128-record landscape-script bank at `game+0xff81a4`.

functions.name: initialize_track_parcel_runtime
functions.description: Constructs one gameplay parcel runtime slot by clearing the shared body object and installing the parcel-slot vtable. `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` uses it to seed the 50-slot array at `game->track_parcels`.

functions.name: abort_startup_with_3d_error
functions.description: Shows the native 3D-accelerator startup error dialog, terminates the shell process, and returns through the shared `cRBod` constructor on the static sentinel object.

functions.name: initialize_bod
functions.description: Constructs one `cRBod` record by clearing its shared list links, installing the body update selector, and incrementing the global Bod-count ledger used by `construct_game_runtime`.

functions.name: load_galaxy_layout
functions.description: Loads `Galaxy/_Galaxy.txt`, rescales the authored galaxy-point tables, and populates the runtime galaxy route records with star counts, authored positions, and placeholder level labels.

functions.name: destroy_galaxy
functions.description: Tears down the active Star Map galaxy-screen widgets before replay launch, route handoff, or return to the owning front-end state. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRGalaxy::UnInit()`.

functions.name: initialize_galaxy
functions.description: Builds the Star Map galaxy screen, seeds its selected route from SnailMail.cfg, and wires the Deliver/Play, replay, and back controls. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRGalaxy::Init()`.

functions.name: update_galaxy
functions.description: Runs the Star Map galaxy-screen state machine, including route selection, replay launch, and Deliver/Play actions through the shared level-path handoff. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRGalaxy::AI()`.

functions.name: close_galaxy_route
functions.description: Closes the currently open Star Map route card, clears its active replay or play controls, and resets the open-route selection before another route is opened. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRGalaxy::BoxOff()`.

functions.name: open_galaxy_route
functions.description: Opens one selected Star Map route card, loads its preview label and widget stack, and toggles the replay-only controls for locked or cleared routes. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRGalaxy::Open(int)`.

functions.name: noop_runtime_callback
functions.description: Shared no-op callback used as the first slot in the root frontend/runtime update table at `g_root_runtime_callback_table`.

functions.name: initialize_frontend_overlay_color_lerp
functions.description: Seeds the secondary full-screen RGBA overlay interpolator stored at `data_4df904 + 0x2a8`.

functions.name: draw_frontend_overlay_color_lerp
functions.description: Advances and draws the secondary full-screen RGBA overlay interpolator stored at `data_4df904 + 0x2a8`.

functions.name: begin_frontend_fade_out
functions.description: Requests the shared front-end black transition overlay to fade from clear to full black and hold before a screen handoff.

functions.name: begin_frontend_fade_in
functions.description: Seeds the shared front-end black transition overlay at full black so the next screen fades in over 18 updates.

functions.name: update_frontend_transition_overlay
functions.description: Updates and draws the shared full-screen black front-end transition overlay, including fade-in, fade-out, three-tick black hold, and the handoff sentinel state.

functions.name: initialize_game_assets_and_world
functions.description: Bootstraps the shared app world, front-end managers, score tables, star field, and the embedded 63-pair path-template bank. Public Path= slots 0..50 are constructed in place at GameRoot+0x1066f2c; slots 51..62 own transition meshes for flagged loop/invert families, HALFPIPE is the direct slot-42 constructor call, and WARP slot 30 remains unbuilt.

functions.name: update_frontend_state_machine
functions.description: Dispatches the active front-end screen state, including menu, options, route map, intro, credits, high scores, gameplay handoff, the 26/27/28 subgame bridge states, and the post-run Thanks For Playing owner at states 29/30.

functions.name: set_backdrop_progress_fraction
functions.description: Stores one normalized progress fraction on the active backdrop controller. Windows `update_subgoldy` drives it from the current track-row payload ratio, and `initialize_backdrop` clears the same field at +0x6c8.

functions.name: set_backdrop_distort
functions.description: Seeds the shared backdrop distortion grid and its per-cell phase, speed, and amplitude terms from the script-side Distort scalar. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRBackdrop::SetDistort(float)`.

functions.name: change_backdrop
functions.description: Binds one parsed background script entry into the shared backdrop renderer, including its texture handles, Distort scalar, and split-vs-single draw mode. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRBackdrop::Change(cRLandscape*, bool)`.

functions.name: change_backdrop_real
functions.description: Commits the pending backdrop selection, refreshes the distortion grid when the world index changes, and copies the current split-vs-single draw flags into the active renderer state. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRBackdrop::ChangeReal()`.

functions.name: initialize_backdrop
functions.description: Initializes the shared backdrop renderer, seeds its texture-transition state, and primes its distortion grid. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRBackdrop::Init(int)`.

functions.name: set_backdrop_texture_target
functions.description: Captures the current and next backdrop texture ids and resets the internal crossfade counters inside the shared backdrop renderer.

functions.name: draw_split_backdrop
functions.description: Queues the split `_A` / `_B` backdrop textures directly into the sprite renderer when a background script points at a split menu or splash layout.

functions.name: render_backdrop
functions.description: Draws the single-texture 320x240 backdrop crop through the animated distortion grid. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRBackdrop::Render()`.

functions.name: update_backdrop
functions.description: Advances the shared backdrop renderer, updates its distortion grid, and dispatches the split or warped draw path. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRBackdrop::AI()`.

functions.name: direct3d_renderer_set_cull_mode
functions.description: Renderer-wrapper method that maps the one-bit cull selector to Direct3D render state 0x16 values before object rendering.

functions.name: initialize_game_window_and_input_wrapper
functions.description: Small cdecl wrapper used by startup to forward the SnailMail window title to the window/input initializer body.

functions.name: initialize_game_window_and_input
functions.description: Registers the SnailMail window class, applies the recovered 4:3 resolution presets, falls back to a 640x480 windowed client, and initializes keyboard, controller, and mouse input.

functions.name: uninitialize_input_devices
functions.description: Shuts down the live DirectInput stack by releasing the enumerated controllers, keyboard input, mouse device, and shared DirectInput interface handles.

functions.name: end_overlay_render_state
functions.description: Restores the saved Direct3D projection and view transforms after an orthographic overlay/font render pass.

functions.name: begin_overlay_render_state
functions.description: Saves the current Direct3D projection and view transforms, installs an orthographic overlay projection and translated view matrix, and disables depth/fog state before overlay or font rendering.

functions.name: render_camera
functions.description: Configures one 3D scene viewport, projection, view transform, depth state, fog state, and the after-sprites lane before world objects render. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `G0RenderCamera`.

functions.name: refresh_object_vertex_buffer
functions.description: Refreshes one built object's grouped vertex buffer from the current animated or distorted vertex source, preserving the authored UV lanes for the grouped draw path.

functions.name: render_object_toon
functions.description: Draws one object's optional toon edge pass by transforming the camera delta into object space, selecting boundary or silhouette edges against their two face normals, and submitting the selected vertex pairs as line primitives. The pass uses the global sprite manager for its line texture and the global D3D device for drawing. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `G0RenderToon(cRObject*, tMatrix*)`.

functions.name: render_object
functions.description: Renders one built object through the grouped Direct3D path: refreshes animated vertices, sets world transform and cull mode, binds each texture group, applies the blend preset and tint, submits indexed primitives, and optionally dispatches the toon edge pass. Its third and fourth arguments are the float texture-U and texture-V values forwarded from `BodBase +0x1c/+0x20`. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `G0RenderObject(cRObject*, tMatrix*, float, float, tColourSmall*, bool)`.

functions.name: update_display_mode_view_state
functions.description: Updates the shared display-mode view-sample state against the authored view dimensions before the frame render pass.

functions.name: set_cull_mode
functions.description: Programs the shared renderer cull state from the one-bit front-vs-back-face selection. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `G0SetCull(int)`.

functions.name: load_registered_texture_refs
functions.description: Allocates the Direct3D texture slot array, forwards its legacy mode argument while loading each registered texture reference, and reports the estimated texture memory budget.

functions.name: load_registered_texture_ref
functions.description: Loads one registered texture reference from the archive or filesystem, installs its Direct3D texture slot, derives color-key and texture-stage behavior from the TGA bits-per-pixel header field, and records the loaded dimensions. Its forwarded legacy second argument is unused.

functions.name: set_blend_mode
functions.description: Maps one render blend preset through the shared lookup table and programs the matching alpha-blend state. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `G0SetBlend(int)`.

functions.name: set_immediate_blend_mode
functions.description: Programs the immediate 2D quad alpha-blend render state selected by draw_textured_quad_immediate.

functions.name: draw_textured_quad_immediate
functions.description: Builds one immediate 2D textured quad in the transient UI vertex buffer, supporting explicit corner coordinates, axis-aligned size, or centered rotation before submitting the quad as a six-index primitive.

functions.name: render_game_frame_scene
functions.description: Clears the backbuffer, opens a Direct3D scene, updates display-mode view state, renders one game frame, and closes the scene.

functions.name: present_backbuffer
functions.description: Presents the Direct3D backbuffer with null source, destination, window, and dirty-region arguments.

functions.name: begin_sprite_depth_render_state
functions.description: Saves the active Direct3D view transform, installs identity view and texture transforms, and prepares texture-stage state before render_game_frame depth-sorts sprites.

functions.name: end_sprite_depth_render_state
functions.description: Restores the Direct3D view transform saved by begin_sprite_depth_render_state after the sprite depth bucket pass.

functions.name: configure_sprite_render_state
functions.description: Chooses the sprite render-state preset from the sprite draw-mode lane at `+0x28` before one sprite quad is submitted.

functions.name: draw_sprite_quad
functions.description: Builds and submits one textured sprite quad from the sprite color, scale, angle, and stretch lanes. The star-field pass at `initialize_star_field` and `update_star_positions` reaches this helper through the shared sprite renderer.

functions.name: get_or_append_object_texture_group_vertex
functions.description: Returns the existing grouped-render vertex matching one object vertex index plus UVs, or appends a new record field-by-field through the shared global texture-group build scratch when no match exists.

functions.name: build_object_texture_group_buffers
functions.description: Allocates one Object's texture-group tables, expands each Object-owned face run through six advancing index lanes, uploads the grouped scratch's scalar diffuse plus aggregate UV/XYZ render payload, and retains the resulting Direct3D vertex and index buffers. iOS RObject.o names the owner family `cRObject::BuildGLVertexArray()`.

functions.name: set_object_color
functions.description: Packs one object RGBA tint and, when grouped vertex buffers exist, rewrites the per-vertex diffuse colour lane so the next object draw uses that colour. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper's role to `G0SetColour(tColourSmall*)`.

functions.name: set_fullscreen_mode
functions.description: Switches the renderer's display mode between windowed (`0`) and fullscreen (`1`) through the shared device wrapper, updates the saved window rectangle bookkeeping, and recenters the cursor after the mode change.

functions.name: bind_texture_ref
functions.description: Binds one texture ref, skips redundant binds through the cached current-texture lane, and applies the texture-stage wrap-vs-clamp state from the ref flags. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `G0BindTexture(int)`.

functions.name: replace_object_group_texture_refs
functions.description: Rewrites one built object's grouped-render texture-ref table from an old texture reference to a replacement after grouped buffers have been built.

functions.name: reset_render_counters
functions.description: Clears the per-frame renderer counters for submitted triangles, DrawPrimitive calls, and texture binds before render_game_frame starts.

functions.name: update_golb_ai
functions.description: Advances one live Golb shot actor, including straight-flight or path-follow motion, collision transitions, burst or smoke aftermath, and the final cleanup path once it passes the player or leaves the valid band.

functions.name: create_golb
functions.description: Initializes one Golb shot actor from the player's current movement_flags family and emitter slot, choosing the matching spawn anchor, velocity lane, render owner, and any path-follow state before the actor enters `update_golb_ai`.

functions.name: spawn_golb_trail_sprite
functions.description: Allocates one short-lived trail sprite for the straight-flight Golb family at the supplied world position. `update_golb_ai` emits it in a short three-point chain behind the projectile each tick.

functions.name: spawn_golb_impact_sprite
functions.description: Allocates the short-lived impact sprite used when a Golb shot is killed or reaches a terminal collision point, seeded from the supplied world position.

functions.name: append_subgame_contact_target
functions.description: Appends one garbage or slug contact target to the per-frame subgame contact registry unless the target object's flags include 0x1000.

functions.name: initialize_challenge_setup_screen
functions.description: Builds the mode-1 front-end setup screen with Select Difficulty, Select Speed, Play, Watch Replay, and Back controls before challenge gameplay starts.

functions.name: destroy_challenge_setup_screen
functions.description: Tears down the mode-1 front-end setup screen widgets, including the optional replay control and the difficulty or speed selectors.

functions.name: update_challenge_setup_screen
functions.description: Runs the mode-1 front-end setup screen, including difficulty and speed changes, replay launch, Play, and Back handoff into `update_subgame` state 1.

functions.name: initialize_help_screen
functions.description: Starts MAINMENU.OGG, loads Help.txt into the shared backdrop renderer, and allocates the single Back control. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRHelp::Init()`.

functions.name: destroy_help_screen
functions.description: Clears the Help-screen shell-font scene before control returns to the owning front-end state.

functions.name: update_help_screen
functions.description: Handles the Help-screen Back action, tears down the shell-font scene, and returns control to the New Game front-end flow via state `2`. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRHelp::AI()`.

functions.name: initialize_high_score_screen
functions.description: Builds the postal or challenge high-score screen, optionally enables inline name entry for a pending rank, and lays out the per-row replay or submit controls. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRHighScore::Init(int, int)`.

functions.name: destroy_high_score_screen
functions.description: Tears down the active high-score table widgets, preserves the selected bank index, and resets the shell-font scene before control returns to the owning front-end state. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRHighScore::UnInit()`.

functions.name: update_high_score_screen
functions.description: Runs the high-score screen state machine, including replay launch, bank toggling, inline name submission, and back or exit actions. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRHighScore::AI()`.

functions.name: initialize_high_score_tables
functions.description: Seeds the postal, challenge, and completion high-score banks with blank cRSubHighScore records before ScoreA/B/C overlays are loaded.

functions.name: load_high_scores_from_file
functions.description: Loads one encoded ScoreA/B/C high-score overlay, decodes compact records, and dispatches each record into the postal, survival, or per-route time-trial bank. The function is side-effect-only; known callers ignore the incidental register return.

functions.name: add_arcade_high_score
functions.description: Attempts to insert one completed postal or arcade run into the ordered top-10 bank, then arms the high-score entry screen when the run places. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubHighScore::AddArcade(int)`.

functions.name: add_survival_high_score
functions.description: Attempts to insert one completed challenge or survival run into the ordered top-10 bank, mirrors the pending entry into the challenge bank overlay, and arms the high-score entry screen when the run places. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubHighScore::AddSurvival()`.

functions.name: add_time_trial_high_score
functions.description: Records one per-route time-trial result into the completion bank, replacing the stored entry only when the new time beats the existing route record. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubHighScore::AddTimeTrial(int, bool)`.

functions.name: save_high_scores_and_config
functions.description: Serializes active postal, survival, and time-trial high-score records into ScoreA.dat, ScoreB.dat, and ScoreC.dat according to the save mask, then writes the shared SnailMail.cfg blob.

functions.name: initialize_high_score_entry
functions.description: Clears one cRSubHighScore record, seeds its score-mode metadata, and copies the current player-name buffer into the entry name field.

functions.name: mini_delete_high_score_entry
functions.description: Deletes a canceled pending top-ten row by shifting later records upward through the active bank. Cross-port iOS symbols match this owner method to `cRSubHighScore::MiniDelete(int)`.

functions.name: exit_high_score_screen
functions.description: Leaves the high-score screen, restores front-end state `2` for postal or `10` for challenge, and seeds rebuild selector `2` on both paths. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRHighScore::Exit()`.

functions.name: initialize_new_game_menu
functions.description: Builds the New Game submenu, including Tutorial, Postal Mode, Time Trial, Challenge Mode, Help, and Back, after reloading the shared menu backdrop and shell-font scene. The menu-local replay-attract controller at `data_4df904 + 0x4f2dc` survives this init; direct button handlers later use frontend state `10`, with Postal Mode also seeding the ordinary rebuild selector `+0x12e55e0 = 2`.

functions.name: update_new_game_menu
functions.description: Runs the New Game menu controller, dispatching Tutorial, Postal Mode, Time Trial, Challenge Mode, Help, and Back through the shared front-end state machine. It also advances the menu-owned random replay-attract lane: rotates cursor `0..4`, probes replay banks `0/1/3` only, gives up after `1000` attempts, seeds app replay scratch `+0x1066be8/+0x1066be9/+0x1066bec/+0x1066bf0` plus return state `2`, sets the menu hide latch, and resets the secondary timer lane to `1/3600` after either launch or miss.

functions.name: load_landscape_script_by_name
functions.description: Loads or reuses one `Backgrounds/<name>` landscape script, parses its `ID`, `Fog`, `Picture`, `Landscape`, and `Distort` fields, registers the referenced backdrop textures, and returns the cached landscape index.

functions.name: activate_landscape_entry
functions.description: Selects one cached landscape script, activates up to ten repeated 0x90-byte landscape BOD slices for its object index, chooses the backdrop variant flag, and copies the script fog color into global render state.

functions.name: clear_active_landscape_entries
functions.description: Clears the active 10-slot landscape-entry runtime pool by zeroing each live slot state and unlinking it from the shared BOD active/free list. `destroy_subgame` uses it when the current landscape set is torn down.

functions.name: update_active_landscape_entry
functions.description: Updates one active repeated landscape slice: state 1 wraps its inherited transform z offset by the recovered repeat span, then toggles the shared visibility bit against the reference BOD camera band.

functions.name: initialize_loading_screen
functions.description: Loads the loading-screen textures, seeds the full-screen quad and progress-bar quad, and resets the loader progress counters.

functions.name: destroy_loading_screen
functions.description: Tears down the loading-screen textures and preserves the last measured loading budget for the next loader pass.

functions.name: update_loading_screen
functions.description: Advances the normalized loader percentage, draws the loading background, and clips the LoadingBarOn fill quad into the recovered 192x289 to 448x321 window.

functions.name: initialize_intro_screen
functions.description: Loads SpaceRed plus INTROTEXT.OGG, parses the intro or credits script into centered text and image renderables on the tilted crawl plane, and derives the shared scroll delta from Duration. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRIntro::Init()`.

functions.name: destroy_intro_screen
functions.description: Unlinks each intro or credits crawl renderable from the active body lists and returns it to the shared free list before the text screen exits. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRIntro::UnInit()`.

functions.name: update_intro_screen
functions.description: Advances the normalized intro or credits crawl, tears down its renderables once the scroll completes, and exits early on skip input. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRIntro::AI()`.

functions.name: update_intro_logo_renderable
functions.description: Advances one intro/credits or logo renderable by applying its velocity to the shared BOD position, fades alpha as it passes the z-window, marks the backing object dirty, and toggles the visible bit. The old `update_logo_row` name was too narrow: `initialize_intro_screen` uses the same 0x90-byte bank for intro text and image renderables, while `open_logo` uses the 32-slot logo bank.

functions.name: destroy_main_menu
functions.description: Clears the active menu shell-font scene and tears down the current main-menu widget set before control leaves the front-end. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match the owning flow to `cRMainMenu::UnInit()`.

functions.name: initialize_main_menu
functions.description: Starts MAINMENU.OGG, loads Menubg.txt into the shared backdrop renderer, seeds the front-end state to menu mode, and allocates the New Game, High Scores, Options, Credits, and Exit widgets. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRMainMenu::Init()`.

functions.name: update_main_menu
functions.description: Handles main-menu selection, front-end screen transitions, and the tutorial launch handoff. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRMainMenu::AI()`.

functions.name: sort_object_faces_by_texture_group
functions.description: Sorts one Object's 0x30-byte face records into contiguous texture runs using independent scan and insertion cursors, swapping whole ObjectFaceQuad records so calc_object_texture_groups can retain cumulative run ends.

functions.name: initialize_textured_backdrop_quad
functions.description: Builds one shared 4-vertex textured backdrop quad with full-range UVs for the `Objects/Univers*` lane.

functions.name: raise_backdrop_quad_edge_pair
functions.description: Raises one of the two adjacent backdrop-quad edge pairs by 0.5 units using the small orientation tables consumed by the `Objects/Univers` variants.

functions.name: initialize_backdrop_slice_quad
functions.description: Builds one shared 4-vertex textured backdrop quad using a horizontal 1/8 UV slice selected by the panel index for the `Objects/World00*` lane.

functions.name: initialize_backdrop_corner_quad
functions.description: Builds one sliced backdrop quad and offsets the selected edge pair by 0.3 units using the four-entry corner selector table.

functions.name: sample_smtrack_heightmap
functions.description: Samples one source texture into the active SMTrack float-height grid by averaging the source RGB channels, applying the optional cubic curve, and scaling the result into the destination height lane.

functions.name: initialize_backdrop_tile_quad
functions.description: Builds one oriented textured backdrop tile quad by choosing one of the eight UV-orientation cases and offsetting the local tile with the supplied row and column selectors.

functions.name: reset_spring_float
functions.description: Clears the five-float spring state used by the player's visual root before initializing SubGoldy.

functions.name: initialize_options_menu
functions.description: Constructs the Options screen widgets, seeds them from SnailMail.cfg, and links the fullscreen, sounds, music, and back controls. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cROptions::Init()`.

functions.name: destroy_options_menu
functions.description: Tears down the Options-screen widgets and writes the current SnailMail.cfg blob back to disk. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cROptions::UnInit()`.

functions.name: update_options_menu
functions.description: Runs the Options-screen state machine, including fullscreen toggles, slider updates, audio-volume refresh, save, and return to the owning front-end state. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cROptions::AI()`.

functions.name: apply_audio_config_volumes
functions.description: Pushes the saved sound and music scalar fields from SnailMail.cfg into the audio engine.

functions.name: allocate_path_template_samples
functions.description: Allocates the primary and secondary `PathTemplateSample` arrays for one path template and stores both pointers on the template object. Constructors treat it as a pure allocator; the old returned secondary-sample pointer is not meaningfully consumed.

functions.name: initialize_looptheloop_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the LoopTheLoop attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildLoopTheLoop(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_looptheloopw_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the wide LoopTheLoop attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildLoopTheLoopW(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_loopout_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the LoopOut attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildLoopOut(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_hump_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Hump attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildHump(float, float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_dump_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Dump attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildDump(float, float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_dip_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Dip attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildDip(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_screw_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Screw attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildScrew(int, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_slalom_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Slalom attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildSlalom(int, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_worm_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Worm attachment path template; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildWorm(char*)`.

functions.name: begin_track_attachment_follow_state
functions.description: Starts one live attachment-follow session from the selected runtime attachment cell by marking the follow struct active, storing the template and owner cell, zeroing the segment index, seeding progress from `world_z - cell_anchor_z`, seeding local height from `world_y - 0.49`, caching the player pointer, and copying the row payload into template `+0x98`. Windows `cdb` confirmed shipped `ARCADE007` HalfPipe entries hit this helper with entry progress around `0.03595` and near-zero entry height. iOS Path.o names the player-follow owner family `cRPathFollowGoldy::Init(cRSubLoc*, tVector&, cRSubGoldy*)`.

functions.name: update_track_attachment_follow_state
functions.description: Advances one live attachment-follow session along the current template, updates segment, progress, and local height, writes the interpolated output transform back into the follow struct, and returns a small mode code consumed by `update_subgoldy`. Windows `cdb` confirmed `ARCADE007` produces mid-follow samples with local height `-0.49f`, and the case-1 or case-3 return path feeds one direct fall-state handoff inside `update_subgoldy`. Raw BN plus IDA also show the nearby `voice 4` call guarded by `sample_index + 1 == template->sample_count << 1`, which conflicts with the same helper's `sample_index == template->sample_count` termination under the current typed counters, so that milestone lane remains unresolved rather than ported. iOS Path.o names the player-follow advance family `cRPathFollowGoldy::Traverse(float, tVector&, tVector*)`.

functions.name: initialize_path_follow_golb
functions.description: Starts one Golb projectile attachment-follow session; iOS Path.o names this owner family `cRPathFollowGolb::Init(cRSubLoc*, tVector&, cRSubGolb*)`.

functions.name: calc_path_length_z
functions.description: Advances one Golb projectile attachment-follow session and emits the interpolated position or exit velocity. iOS Path.o names the matching follow-state advance family `cRPathFollowGolb::Traverse(float, tVector&, tVector*)`; the path-template length finalizer is separately exposed as `cRPath::CalcLengthZ()`.

functions.name: mirror_path_template_pair_x
functions.description: Clones one path-template pair, negates X-space samples, and flips strip winding for the mirrored half. iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::Mirror(cRPath*)`.

functions.name: initialize_slalombig_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the SlalomBig attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildSlalomBig(int, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_sweep_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Sweep attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildSweep(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_snake_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Snake attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildSnake(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_supertramp_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the SuperTramp attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildSuperTramp(float, float, int, bool, char*, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: build_track_fringe_mesh
functions.description: Builds the generated side/fringe strip mesh for one attachment path template; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildFringe(char*, float)`.

functions.name: build_track_fringe_supertramp_mesh
functions.description: Builds the generated SuperTramp fringe strip mesh for one attachment path template; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildFringeSuperTramp(char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_slalomdouble_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the SlalomDouble attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildSlalomDouble(int, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_p_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the P attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildP(int, float, int, float, float, int, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_start_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Start attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildStart(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_turnover_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Turnover attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildTurnover(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_turnoverdouble_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the TurnoverDouble attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildTurnoverDouble(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_turnunder_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Turnunder attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildTurnunder(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_wibble_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Wibble attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildWibble(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_invert_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Invert attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o has both `cRPath::BuildInvert(float, int, bool, char*, char*)` and `cRPath::BuildInvert1(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`, so this Windows split keeps the conservative family name.

functions.name: find_segment_path_index_by_name
functions.description: Linearly searches the hardcoded 51-entry Path= name table and returns the matching index or -1.

functions.name: initialize_halfpipe_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Windows public HALFPIPE path-template pair at authored slot 42. The direct initialize_game_assets_and_world call targets pair base game+0x106a64c, seeds runtime kind 42 with 66 samples, and emits the generated strip mesh through the shared kind-42 transform helper; Android cRPath::BuildHalfPipe independently matches the shape.

functions.name: initialize_twister_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the TwisterA attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildTwisterA(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_twister2_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Twister2A attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildTwister2A(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: compute_kind42_attachment_transform
functions.description: Computes the nonlinear temporary transform used by attachment kind 42 during projection, live follow, and constructor-side strip generation. Android's cRPath::HalfPipePos is a strong structural match, but the exact Windows public-path naming for this family remains open.

functions.name: get_path_position_at_node
functions.description: Projects a path node and row index into local/output position for attachment-row consumers; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::GetPos(tVector&, int, int, tVector&)`.

functions.name: initialize_loopbow_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the LoopBow attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildLoopBow(float, int, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: finalize_path_template
functions.description: Finalizes one built path template after constructor or mirror edits by recomputing derived per-sample lengths, inverting sample transforms, updating the derived sample scalar at `+0xa4`, and normalizing the nested strip-mesh state. iOS Path.o names this finalizer family `cRPath::CalcLengthZ()`.

functions.name: try_enter_track_attachment_from_swept_motion
functions.description: Scans the authored runtime attachment samples against the player's swept motion box, seeds the shared scratch follow-state block when a candidate hit survives the local bounds tests, and immediately runs one follow update to validate the candidate. Windows `cdb` confirmed shipped `ARCADE007` HalfPipe play reaches the live follow-state path driven by this entry family. Raw BN plus the checked-in IDA export now also show the helper itself does not directly clear `player + 0x41d` before control returns to the caller's secondary-slot gate.

functions.name: is_point_inside_track_attachment
functions.description: Tests whether one probe/swept-motion pair lies inside the selected attachment template cell. iOS Path.o names the closest split helper `cRPath::SearchPos(tVector, tVector, cRSubLoc*)`.

functions.name: initialize_toad_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Toad attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildToad(bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_hill_valley_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Hill/Valley attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this family `cRPath::BuildHill(int, float, float, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_sbend_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the SBend attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildSBend(int, float, float, bool, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_cage2_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds the Cage2 attachment path-template pair; iOS Path.o names this `cRPath::BuildCage2(int, char*, char*)`.

functions.name: load_file_bytes_from_path
functions.description: Loads one filesystem path into caller-supplied or freshly allocated bytes, optionally returning the byte count through the extra output lanes.

functions.name: validate_config_tail_stub
functions.description: Legacy config-tail validation helper called after SnailMail.cfg is loaded; this build ignores the tail pointer and returns true.

functions.name: is_bod_after_sprites
functions.description: Returns whether one body should draw after sprites; iOS RObject.o names this `cRBod::IsAfterSprites()`.

functions.name: set_bod_object
functions.description: Stores the render-object pointer on one body record; iOS RObject.o names this `cRBod::SetObject(cRObject*)`.

functions.name: initialize_bod_base
functions.description: Initializes the shared bod header and default state: noop runtime AI callback, default flags, white color block, cleared linkage fields, and the global bod counter increment. iOS RObject.o names this base constructor `cRBod::cRBod()`.

functions.name: initialize_renderable_bod
functions.description: Builds the shared renderable bod shell on top of the base bod header, seeding its object-oriented vtable, default render flags, and identity local transform. iOS RObject.o names this positioned-body constructor `cRBodPos::cRBodPos()`.

functions.name: apply_bod_position
functions.description: Applies one body's position into the supplied matrix before object rendering; iOS RObject.o names this `cRBod::ApplyPos(tMatrix&)`.

functions.name: initialize_object
functions.description: Constructs one render object with empty geometry buffers and default render flags; iOS RObject.o names this `cRObject::cRObject()`.

functions.name: request_object_vertices
functions.description: Ensures one render object has the requested vertex storage; iOS RObject.o names this `cRObject::RequestVertices(int)`.

functions.name: request_object_vertices_copy
functions.description: Ensures one render object has the secondary copied-vertex storage; iOS RObject.o names this `cRObject::RequestVerticesCopy()`.

functions.name: request_object_facequad_normals
functions.description: Ensures one object's toon-normal storage exists by allocating the per-vertex accumulated normal list and the per-face quad-normal pairs consumed by the outline pass.

functions.name: request_object_facequads
functions.description: Ensures one render object has the requested face-quad storage; iOS RObject.o names this `cRObject::RequestFaceQuads(int)`.

functions.name: initialize_object_list
functions.description: Initializes the global render-object list with the requested capacity; iOS RObject.o names this `cRObjects::Init(int)`.

functions.name: build_all_objects
functions.description: Builds every object in the global render-object list; iOS RObject.o names this `cRObjects::BuildObjects()`.

functions.name: apply_object_toon
functions.description: Marks one object as toon-enabled and prepares its outline support buffers. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRObject::ApplyToon(int)`, but the Windows split also allocates support storage here.

functions.name: add_object_to_list
functions.description: Appends one render object to the global object list and returns the new slot; iOS RObject.o names this `cRObjects::Add()`.

functions.name: calc_object_bounding_box
functions.description: Recomputes one cRObject's min/max bounds and maximum vertex magnitude in place. Its sole native caller discards EAX, confirming this is a void object mutator rather than the decompiler-derived count-returning helper.

functions.name: calc_object_facequad_normals
functions.description: Builds one object's toon-normal data: face-pair normals per quad plus averaged inverted vertex normals for silhouette edge tests. Called from the object build path and again when animated geometry is refreshed. iOS RObject.o names this `cRObject::CalcFaceQuadNormals()`.

functions.name: calc_object_facequad_normals_simple
functions.description: Recomputes only the per-face quad-normal pairs for the current vertex positions without rebuilding the full accumulated vertex-normal table. Used by the distort path before the toon edge pass reuses the updated face data.

functions.name: calc_object_texture_groups
functions.description: Collapses contiguous face quads by texture group for one render object; iOS RObject.o names this `cRObject::CalcTextureGroups()`.

functions.name: advance_frame_sequence
functions.description: Advances one frame-sequence block by its fractional step, handling wrap, restart, reverse, and ping-pong flags before publishing the current frame payload pointer.

functions.name: request_object_edges
functions.description: Allocates the packed toon-edge records and companion selection buffer that `render_object_toon` consumes when it emits silhouette line segments.

functions.name: add_object_edge
functions.description: Adds or merges one candidate toon edge in the global build scratch array. Its two vertex members are 32-bit indices; new records store a normalized direction plus its original length, while shared records add the second face normal and may be removed when the join should not outline. iOS RObject.o names this `cRObject::AddEdge(int, int, int)`.

functions.name: calc_object_edges
functions.description: Builds the unique edge list for one toon-enabled object from its face quads, optionally removes boundary edges for closed meshes, and stores the packed records later filtered by `render_object_toon`. iOS RObject.o names this `cRObject::CalcEdges()`.

functions.name: rotate_object_facequad_uv_pairs
functions.description: Rotates one ObjectFaceQuad's four UV pairs forward by one slot; used by backdrop tile orientation cases before writing the matching quad vertices.

functions.name: request_object_animation
functions.description: Ensures one render object has generated animation-frame storage; iOS RObject.o names this `cRObject::RequestAnim(int, cRBodPos*, float, int)`.

functions.name: replace_object_list_texture_refs
functions.description: Walks the global object list and replaces one texture reference with another across face quads and any already-built grouped-render texture tables.

functions.name: uninitialize_game_data_archive
functions.description: Frees the tracked archive payload buffer, closes the loaded SnailMail.dat stream when present, and clears the global archive-index pointer.

functions.name: set_current_directory_with_drive_fallback
functions.description: Wraps `SetCurrentDirectoryA`, retrying drive-qualified paths through the Win32 current-drive environment-variable fallback before emitting a plain debug message on failure.

functions.name: load_file_bytes_fixed_size_from_archive_or_fs
functions.description: Loads exactly the caller-supplied byte count from a named archive entry or filesystem path, reusing the caller buffer when provided and otherwise allocating a tracked buffer before applying the archive XOR decode.

functions.name: delete_file_path_with_directory_walk
functions.description: Deletes one filesystem path after walking each parent directory component through the shared current-directory helper, mirroring the save-path builder beside it; used by the temporary BASS DLL teardown path.

functions.name: enumerate_matching_archive_or_fs_entries
functions.description: Enumerates archive-backed or filesystem-backed filenames under the requested prefix, filters them against the caller wildcard pattern, copies each match into the fixed output table, and returns the match count through the out parameter.

functions.name: xor_archive_bytes_in_place
functions.description: Applies the archive byte XOR mask in place over the requested byte span; the same transform is used to decode archive reads and to encode rebuilt archive payloads.

functions.name: load_archive_index
functions.description: Decodes and parses the SnailMail.dat archive index into lookup records for archive-backed file loading.

functions.name: is_archive_index_loaded
functions.description: Returns whether the global archive-index table has been initialized, so content loaders can decide between archive-backed reads and direct filesystem fallback.

functions.name: get_archive_data_base
functions.description: Returns the base pointer of the loaded SnailMail.dat payload buffer used by archive-backed mesh, voice, and object-definition readers.

functions.name: get_archive_data_end
functions.description: Returns the end pointer of the fixed-size loaded archive payload window; current Windows callers use it as the upper bound when walking archive-backed texture-group buffers.

functions.name: get_tracked_allocation_size
functions.description: Looks up one tracked allocation inside the global allocation stack and returns the guarded byte size recorded for that pointer.

functions.name: push_tracked_allocation
functions.description: Appends one `(size, pointer)` record to the global tracked-allocation stack used by the guarded resource allocator.

functions.name: pop_tracked_allocation
functions.description: Removes one pointer from the global tracked-allocation stack, preferring the top-of-stack fast path and falling back to a linear search when the free order differs.

functions.name: allocate_tracked_memory
functions.description: Allocates a zeroed guarded heap block, records it in the global tracked-allocation stack, writes the front and back `DE AD BA BE` sentinels, and returns the caller-visible payload pointer.

functions.name: free_tracked_memory
functions.description: Validates a guarded tracked allocation, removes its size from the global memory tally, frees the backing block, and drops the pointer from the tracked-allocation stack.

functions.name: free_tracked_allocations_to_mark
functions.description: Frees tracked allocations back down to the current saved stack mark, as used by the main loop to unwind one temporary allocation scope.

functions.name: set_tracked_allocation_mark
functions.description: Saves the current tracked-allocation stack depth as the active unwind mark for a later `free_tracked_allocations_to_mark` call.

functions.name: report_errorf
functions.description: Formats and emits one fatal-style engine error message with a varargs payload. Runtime and content-loading paths use it for hard failures such as missing hotspot textures, parcel-pool exhaustion, and invalid track segment lengths.

functions.name: report_warningf
functions.description: Formats one warning message with the native `* WARNING *` prefix and emits it through the shared debug-output path.

functions.name: report_messagef
functions.description: Formats and emits one plain debug message without the warning prefix; archive/file loading paths use it for missing-file notices.

functions.name: copy_c_string
functions.description: Copies one NUL-terminated C string into the destination buffer and returns the advanced source pointer.

functions.name: strings_equal_case_insensitive
functions.description: Compares two strings case-insensitively and returns true when the right-hand string terminates after matching, making it a prefix-friendly tag/path comparator.

functions.name: skip_to_next_line
functions.description: Advances the caller-owned text cursor to the next line break and then steps once more so the cursor starts on the following line.

functions.name: append_c_string
functions.description: Advances to the destination string's trailing NUL and appends the source string in place.

functions.name: parse_next_space_delimited_token
functions.description: Skips leading spaces, copies the next space-delimited token from the caller-owned text cursor into the destination buffer, and advances the cursor.

functions.name: parse_next_int32
functions.description: Parses the next signed decimal integer from the caller-owned text cursor, skipping leading spaces and advancing the cursor past the consumed digits.

functions.name: parse_next_float32
functions.description: Parses the next signed decimal float from the caller-owned text cursor, consuming digits and one decimal point before returning the float32 value.

functions.name: set_input_controller_slot0_button_axes
functions.description: Stores the keyboard-derived button mask and X/Y axis floats into input-controller slot 0, overwriting its shared digital and analog lanes before later pointer data is merged in.

functions.name: update_input_controller_slot_button_axes
functions.description: Updates one global input-controller slot from a button mask and two analog axes. Slot 0 merges button bits and only fills empty analog lanes, while nonzero slots overwrite their lane directly and derive the slot's centered authored-X value from the X axis with the native 0.15 deadzone.

functions.name: copy_active_input_controller_state
functions.description: Copies one of the two global input-controller slots into the caller-owned runtime buffer, including button flags, normalized pointer coordinates, and the per-slot authored cursor lanes used by gameplay input.

functions.name: update_input_controller_pointer_region
functions.description: Refreshes one global input-controller slot from a screen-space pointer region: stores the active bounds, optionally warps the pixel cursor into that region, updates normalized authored 640x480 coordinates, and latches the per-slot button bits.

functions.name: set_input_controller_pointer_authored_xy
functions.description: Seeds one global input-controller slot to an authored 640x480 pointer position, updates its normalized coordinates from the stored region bounds, and warps the backing OS cursor through click_mouse_screen.

functions.name: read_pressed_text_input_key_code
functions.description: Maps newly pressed keyboard scancodes into the shared text-input byte codes used by frontend widgets, cheat entry, intro skip, and subgame ESC handling: ASCII for letters/digits/punctuation plus the small control-code set for edit and navigation keys.

functions.name: read_repeating_text_input_key_code
functions.description: Maps held keyboard scancodes into the same text-input byte codes as read_pressed_text_input_key_code, but with the native repeat delay/rate state so border_input_text can autorepeat held edits and cursor moves.

functions.name: register_font_texture_sheet_wrapper
functions.description: Small cdecl wrapper used by startup to forward the FONT-MENU-HOVER sheet path, font kind, width scale, and height scale into `register_font_texture_sheet`.

functions.name: cache_music_file
functions.description: Ensures one `music/*.ogg` payload is resident in the shared music-file cache, loading it from the archive or filesystem on first use and reusing the cached bytes afterward.

functions.name: play_sound_effect_backend
functions.description: Shared SFX backend wrapper that plays one effect id with an explicit gain scale plus optional extra playback parameters before forwarding into the lower sound-bank calls.

functions.name: play_voice_backend
functions.description: Shared voice backend wrapper that plays one voice sample id with an explicit gain scale plus the same optional extra playback parameters used by the SFX backend.

functions.name: register_sound_sample
functions.description: Registers one voice or sound-bank sample path into the shared 256-entry runtime sound table, loading bytes from the archive on first use and returning the assigned sample index.

functions.name: find_registered_sound_sample_id_by_name
functions.description: Scans the registered sound-sample path/name table populated by `register_sound_sample` and returns the matching sample id for level `Sample="..."` message audio, reporting an error and returning 0 on miss.

functions.name: get_authored_view_width
functions.description: Returns the authored 640-style view width float used while updating display-mode view samples.

functions.name: get_authored_view_height
functions.description: Returns the authored 480-style view height float used while updating display-mode view samples.

functions.name: initialize_track_render_cache_manager
functions.description: Initializes the fixed 143x5 track-render cache bod grid plus its shared GDX cache vertex/index buffers and per-cache texture-group scratch arrays.

functions.name: build_track_render_caches
functions.description: Builds the Floor, Slide, Warn, Ramp, and Fringe track render caches after runtime track normalization.

functions.name: add_track_cache_vertex
functions.description: Transforms and deduplicates one source object vertex into a render-cache family staging buffer, optionally projecting track UVs.

functions.name: append_track_cache_object
functions.description: Appends one source object's triangle or quad faces to a render-cache family by emitting deduplicated vertices and indices.

functions.name: update_track_render_cache_rows
functions.description: Activates forward track-render cache row BODs while Goldy's current row position stays ahead of the next cached row, linking the Floor, Slide, Warn, Ramp, and Fringe slots into their active lists and seeding each slot color.

functions.name: update_active_bod
functions.description: Implements the default active-bod subtype callback by recycling a live bod to the free list once its world-z plus the native 24-unit buffer falls behind the current subgame cutoff.

functions.name: remove_track_render_cache_bods
functions.description: Unlinks the fixed 143x5 track-render cache bod grid from the shared active-bod list during subgame teardown.

functions.name: initialize_thanks_for_playing_screen
functions.description: Initializes the post-run Thanks For Playing owner, including the Splash backdrop, INTROTEXT.OGG, and the first shell-font message widget before state 30 begins updating it.

functions.name: update_thanks_for_playing_screen
functions.description: Runs the post-run Thanks For Playing message sequence, including the Challenge Mode and Time Trial prompts, Click to Continue, and the fade-out handoff back through the saved front-end return state.

functions.name: destroy_star_field
functions.description: Destroys the live star-field sprite pool, tears down its per-entry records, and returns the controller to state 0.

functions.name: open_star_field
functions.description: Allocates the persistent star-field controller storage and, when the global star-pass flag is enabled, immediately initializes the live streak sprite pool for the requested slot count.

functions.name: initialize_star_field
functions.description: Builds the live star-field streak sprites: allocates one sprite per entry, seeds camera-relative positions, randomizes direction and speed, tints them pale blue-white, and primes the per-streak length or fade fields.

functions.name: hide_star_field
functions.description: Clears bit 0x40 on every live star-field sprite, hiding the background star pass without destroying its controller state.

functions.name: unhide_star_field
functions.description: Sets bit 0x40 on every live star-field sprite, re-enabling the background streak pass without rebuilding the controller.

functions.name: update_star_field
functions.description: Runs the four-state star-field controller, fading the streak sprites in or out around the global star-pass flag and dispatching the per-frame camera-relative position update.

functions.name: update_star_positions
functions.description: Advances each star-field streak along its cached direction, resets wrapped entries against the current camera origin, and scales the visible sprite trail by both per-streak speed and the controller fade factor.

functions.name: build_track_fringe_objects
functions.description: Allocates directional fringe objects around runtime cells for the post-build renderer, using the shared fringe-manager pool at `data_4df904 + 0x3d01d4`.

functions.name: merge_track_tile_runs
functions.description: Collapses repeated horizontal tile runs into longer strip variants where the renderer allows it.

functions.name: mark_track_warning_zones
functions.description: Marks warning-zone footprints around hazard-bearing runtime tiles before the fringe-object and cache builders run. Cross-port Android symbols match this pass to `cRSubGame::WarnTrack()`.

functions.name: harmonize_center_lane_floor_slide_variants
functions.description: Normalizes center-lane floor and slide seam variants after the main edge pass. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this pass to `cRSubGame::SlideSmoothTrack()`.

functions.name: select_track_tile_edge_variants
functions.description: Computes neighbor masks and chooses edge or corner variants for special runtime tile classes. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this pass to `cRSubGame::SmoothTrack()`.

functions.name: build_track_colours
functions.description: Builds the active subgame track colour banks used by skirt, fringe, and row-cache rendering. Cross-port iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::BuildColours()` in `SubGame.o`.

functions.name: set_subgame_features
functions.description: Derives the active subgame feature flags from the current mode, level, and challenge setup before runtime rows and pickups are built. Cross-port iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::SetFeatures()` in `SubGame.o`.

functions.name: populate_runtime_track_cells_from_segments
